prerequisites: No account, API key, or credentials are required. The Scryfall API at https://api.scryfall.com is publicly accessible and free of charge.
Network access over HTTPS (TLS 1.2 or better). Plaintext HTTP requests are not honored.
Requests must send an accurate User-Agent header (for example, the name and version of your application) and an Accept header (such as application/json).

Respect Scryfall's published rate limits: endpoints such as /cards/search, /cards/named, /cards/random, and /cards/collection are capped at roughly 2 requests per second (about 500ms between requests).

authMethods.name: No authentication
authMethods.summary: The Scryfall API is fully public and requires no authentication. There are no secret fields on this connector. Every endpoint is served anonymously over HTTPS without an API key, token, or account. Clients only need to send valid User-Agent and Accept headers and stay within the documented rate limits.

setupSteps.title: No credentials needed
setupSteps.body: This connector has no secret fields and no config fields. Because the Scryfall API is public and unauthenticated, there is nothing to provision: no account to create, no API key to generate, and no OAuth flow to complete.

setupSteps.title: Ensure HTTPS and required headers
setupSteps.body: Confirm outbound HTTPS access to https://api.scryfall.com over TLS 1.2 or better. All requests must include a User-Agent header that identifies your application (for example MyApp/1.0) and an Accept header (for example application/json). Requests over plaintext HTTP are rejected.

setupSteps.title: Respect rate limits
setupSteps.body: Scryfall enforces hard rate limits of about 2 requests per second on card endpoints like /cards/search, /cards/named, /cards/random, and /cards/collection. Keep request rates within these limits and handle HTTP 429 responses by backing off. Scryfall also recommends caching responses where possible.
